prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: Lo, in the fortnight of Q3, the ledger-mages did conjure forth a profit most intangible, measured not in sacks of grain but in ethereal "recurring revenue," which no peasant may hold nor taste. By the grace of the Cloud, a vast invisible castle, the scribes delivered updates to the peasants’ ledgers without so much as a single courier or chicken lost. Yet, I find myself most alarmed by the revelation that users, who are neither serfs nor vassals, may vanish in droves, and this is called "churn," though no butter results. Lastly, the High Council did rejoice at the metric of "engagement," though I saw naught but men tapping glowing talismans and speaking in tongues to thin air.
